full_text_rationale: Full text describes CoEvoSkills constructing persistent multi-file skill packages for Claude Code and Codex, accumulating verifier feedback across iterations and transferring evolved reusable task structures to fresh coding-agent runs.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Hanrong Zhang, Shicheng Fan, Henry Peng Zou, Yankai Chen, Zhenting Wang, Jiayu Zhou, Chengze Li, Wei-Chieh Huang, Yifei Yao, Kening Zheng, Xue Liu, Xiaoxiao Li, Philip S. Yu
titulo: CoEvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
formato_armazenamento: Persistent structured multi-file skill packages with SKILL.md, instructions, scripts, reusable utility functions, and verifier-derived improvement notes; skills are installed for fresh Claude Code/Codex agents and updated across evolution rounds.
framework_agente: CoEvoSkills co-evolutionary skill-generation framework for Claude Code and Codex
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.2, Claude Code, Codex, six additional transfer LLMs
benchmarks_utilizados: SkillsBench: 87 tasks across roughly 20 professional domains and 11 reported domain groups
metricas_desempenho: Pass rate, delta versus no-skill/human-curated baselines, cross-model transfer pass rate, per-domain pass rate, verification-cycle counts, ablation pass rate
baseline_comparado: No-skill baseline, Anthropic Skill-Creator, single-session skill generation, SkillsBench self-generated skills, CoT-guided self-generation, human-curated skills, ablations without surrogate verifier or without evolution
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Skill versions evolve for up to five rounds; persistent skills are written back as reusable packages and transferred to fresh agents and other model families.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; paper states convergence within five rounds keeps evolution cost practical and reports average verification-cycle counts, but no monetary/token cost per task is extracted.
resultado_principal: CoEvoSkills reaches 71.1% pass rate on SkillsBench with Claude Opus 4.6 + Claude Code, versus 30.6% no-skill baseline and 53.5% human-curated skills. It also reports GPT-5.2 self-evolved skills at 69.8% and broad cross-model transfer gains of about 36-44pp.
desempenho_vs_baseline: CoEvoSkills improves +40.5pp over no-skill and +17.6pp over human-curated skills. Skill-Creator reaches 34.1%, single-session generation 32.4%, SkillsBench self-generated 32.0%, and CoT-guided self-generation 30.7%. Removing the surrogate verifier drops pass rate from 71.1% to 41.1%; removing evolution drops to 48.6%.
custo_vs_baseline: NR as monetary/token cost; evolution converges by about round 5 and requires verifier/oracle cycles.
limitacoes: Ablation rows are single runs due to computational cost; skill evolution depends on verifier quality; results are SkillsBench-centered; generated skills may be domain/model sensitive despite transfer gains.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark composition, deterministic oracle tests, surrogate-verifier information isolation, model/harness choices, number of evolution rounds, and computational budget may affect validity.
